description: Known Java-vs-.NET runtime parity divergences. Entries are matched by operation and optional file, fileGlob, category, or categoryGlob.

entries.reason: Encrypted/compressed path reports lower-level unsupported compression diagnostics before Java-compatible decryption semantics.
Image/filter decoder parity gaps: JBIG2 integration, JPX/CMYK sample handling, and sampled image bit-depth support.
Java can load the encrypted fixture while .NET fails in the encrypted/compressed load path, suppressing downstream operation results.
Merge fails because the first encrypted input follows the same lower-level load failure tracked for the encryption parity issue.
Parser header recovery differs from Java for this malformed/header-offset fixture, so downstream operations are missing until load parity is fixed.
Rendering minimal parser fixtures currently throws before producing Java-compatible blank-page output.
Text extraction for minimal parser fixtures currently throws before producing Java-compatible text output.
Encrypted save restrictions need Java-compatible exception classification and diagnostics.
Default PDFTextStripper semantic output still differs from Java for many successful text extraction cases.
Current .NET first-page renders are blank or near-uniform while Java renders non-background content; tracked as regression fixtures for render validity.
Placeholder renders are reported separately until PageDrawer and related rendering gaps are fixed.
Successful render output may be blank or near-blank until rendering validity checks and PageDrawer work land.
Saved PDF bytes are not yet Java-equivalent across the corpus; faithful save output depends on remaining create/edit, font, and writer parity work.
Merged PDF bytes are tracked as a parity ratchet while the corpus expands and merge/save output normalization is defined.
Pattern color space rendering fails until advanced rendering work is implemented.
Encrypted/public-key failures need Java-compatible exception classification and diagnostics.
Encrypted merge failures need Java-compatible exception classification and diagnostics.
